factors.summary: Supply chain health is challenged due to disruptions and increased costs.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic has impacted our factory productivity and supply chain. Certain of our suppliers, particularly in our pool and flow businesses, faced difficulties maintaining operations in light of manufacturing shutdowns and interruptions due to the COVID-19 pandemic, which negatively impacted our production and contributed to an increase in backlog. During the third quarter of 2020, we identified second source suppliers and increased supply for key items in our pool business to reduce the production and capacity challenges we encountered in the second quarter of 2020 as a result of supply chain issues and increased demand.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company is facing significant disruptions and increased costs due to the pandemic, which negatively impacts production and backlog.

factors.summary: Headcount restructuring is occurring through temporary furloughs and hiring freezes.

evidence.text: With the continuing uncertainty in light of the COVID-19 pandemic, we have taken steps across our organization to align costs with lower sales volumes. These steps include ... temporary furloughs and hiring freezes.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Temporary furloughs and hiring freezes indicate cost-cutting measures in response to lower sales and pandemic uncertainty.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices to mitigate inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: The 8.1 percent increase in net sales for Consumer Solutions in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of selective increases in selling prices to mitigate impacts of inflation; and increased sales due to the Aquion and Pelican acquisitions that occurred in February 2019 and other small acquisitions in the fourth quarter of 2019 and during 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices to mitigate inflation, supported by an 8.1% increase in net sales.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to cost reduction initiatives.

evidence.text: The 0.4 percentage point increase in segment income for Consumer Solutions as a percentage of net sales in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of increased sales volume in our pool business; reduction in travel and entertainment, trade show and advertising expenses due to the COVID-19 pandemic and other cost reduction initiatives; impact of Aquion and Pelican acquisitions; and selective increases in selling prices to mitigate the impacts of inflation.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement due to cost reduction initiatives, leading to a 0.4 percentage point increase in segment income.

factors.summary: The company is managing debt effectively, with a focus on deleveraging.

evidence.text: We believe our existing liquidity position, coupled with our currently anticipated operating cash flows, will be sufficient to meet our cash needs arising in the ordinary course of business for the next twelve months. Although the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results is uncertain, we believe we are well-positioned to manage our business and have the ability and sufficient capacity to meet these cash requirements by using available cash, internally generated funds and borrowing under our committed and uncommitted credit facilities. We are committed to maintaining investment grade credit ratings and a solid liquidity position.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Effective management of debt with a focus on deleveraging, maintaining investment-grade ratings.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are maintained through buybacks and dividends.

evidence.text: In 2020, net cash used for financing activities primarily relates to repayment of commercial paper and revolving long-term debt of $117.5 million, repayment of the 3.625% Senior Notes due in 2020 of $74.0 million, $150.2 million of share repurchases and dividend payments of $127.1 million.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Maintaining shareholder returns through buybacks and dividends, totaling significant amounts.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving.

evidence.text: Free cash flow from continuing operations $ 512.1  $ 287.3  $ 410.1  Net cash (used for) provided by operating activities of discontinued operations (0.6) 7.8  (19.0) Capital expenditures of discontinued operations —  —  (7.4) Proceeds from sale of property and equipment of discontinued operations —  —  2.3  Free cash flow $ 511.5  $ 295.1  $ 386.0

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has significantly improved, showing strong cash generation capabilities.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are favorable in certain areas.

evidence.text: The 5.2 percent decrease in net sales for Industrial & Flow Technologies in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of decreased sales volume in our commercial water supply and water disposal, industrial filtration and food and beverage businesses due to less project sales as a result of customers deferring their capital spending and less service and aftermarket revenue driven by reduced consumption and travel restrictions from government-mandated shutdowns and 'shelter-in-place' orders due to the COVID-19 pandemic; and decreased sales volume in our residential and irrigation flow businesses in the first half of 2020 due to market conditions resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements are favorable in certain areas, contributing to net sales performance.

factors.summary: Management is cautious due to economic uncertainties.

evidence.text: Although the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results is uncertain, we believe we are well-positioned to manage our business and have the ability and sufficient capacity to meet these cash requirements by using available cash, internally generated funds and borrowing under our committed and uncommitted credit facilities. We are committed to maintaining investment grade credit ratings and a solid liquidity position.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Management's cautious stance due to economic uncertainties suggests moderate concern.

factors.summary: The company faces potential legal investigations or claims related to its response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

evidence.text: The impacts of the COVID-19 pandemic may limit our ability to reduce our overall operating costs. We have experienced increased costs relating to our efforts to mitigate the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic through, among other things, enhanced sanitization procedures and social-distancing measures we have enacted and will likely continue to enact at our locations around the world in an effort to protect our employees’ health and well-being.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic is expected to have a material negative impact on our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Evidence focuses on operational costs rather than direct legal claims, indicating no clear trend.

factors.summary: General global economic conditions pose a risk due to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic and economic downturns.

evidence.text: General global economic and business conditions affect demand for our products. We compete in various geographic regions and product markets around the world. Among these, the most significant are global industrial, commercial, and residential markets. We have experienced, and expect to continue to experience, fluctuations in revenues and results of operations due to economic and business cycles.
evidence.subsection: General global economic and business conditions affect demand for our products.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant risks from global economic downturns and the pandemic impact.

factors.summary: The order backlog has increased due to supply chain disruptions and higher demand, particularly in the pool business. While the company has taken steps to mitigate production challenges, such as identifying second source suppliers and increasing supply for key items, these efforts have led to higher transportation and labor costs. The backlog is expected to be mostly shipped in 2021.

evidence.text: During the third quarter of 2020, we identified second source suppliers and increased supply for key items in our pool business to reduce the production and capacity challenges we encountered in the second quarter of 2020 as a result of supply chain issues and increased demand. These supply chain and capacity challenges have led to higher transportation and labor costs in order to timely deliver finished goods to our customers.

The increase in our backlog at December 31, 2020 compared to 2019 was mainly a result of increased demand in our pool business. We expect the majority of our backlog at December 31, 2020 will be shipped in 2021.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increased backlog due to higher demand, despite production challenges.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed, with high demand in the pool business due to the COVID-19 pandemic, while other segments experienced reduced demand. However, there are concerns about a potential deceleration in demand due to the global economic slowdown caused by the pandemic.

evidence.text: Within our Consumer Solutions segment, the COVID-19 pandemic has impacted demand in each of our businesses. Our pool business has experienced high demand as consumers sheltered-in-place and have spent more time at home.

The 8.1 percent increase in net sales for Consumer Solutions in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of increased sales volume across several of the product lines in our pool business due to consumers’ desire to invest in their pools and backyards while sheltering-in-place due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

The COVID-19 pandemic has caused a global economic slowdown that may last for a potentially extended duration, and it is possible that it could cause a global recession. Deteriorating economic and political conditions caused by the COVID-19 pandemic, such as increased unemployment, decreases in capital spending, declines in consumer confidence, or economic slowdowns or recessions, could cause a decrease in demand for our products.
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic is expected to have a material negative impact on our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand with growth in pool business but concerns about deceleration.

factors.summary: Volume trends are mixed, with increases in certain segments such as the pool business and filtration product lines, while other segments experience declines.

evidence.text: The 2.0 percent increase in consolidated net sales in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of: • selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases; • increased sales due to the Aquion and Pelican acquisitions in February 2019 and other small acquisitions in our Consumer Solutions segment in the fourth quarter of 2019 and first half of 2020; • volume increase in our Consumer Solutions segment mainly driven by our pool business; • volume increase in our residential and irrigation flow businesses in our Industrial & Flow Technologies segment; and • favorable foreign currency effects in 2020 compared to the prior year.

The 8.1 percent increase in net sales for Consumer Solutions in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of increased sales volume across several of the product lines in our pool business due to consumers’ desire to invest in their pools and backyards while sheltering-in-place due to the COVID-19 pandemic; and higher sales volumes in our residential filtration product lines in North America and China during the second half of 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed volume trends across segments with some increases and declines.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed across segments, with certain areas experiencing volume increases and acquisitions contributing positively, while other segments, particularly Industrial & Flow Technologies, saw a decrease due to reduced sales volumes and service revenues.

evidence.text: The 2.0 percent increase in consolidated net sales in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of: • selective increases in selling prices to mitigate inflationary cost increases; • increased sales due to the Aquion and Pelican acquisitions in February 2019 and other small acquisitions in our Consumer Solutions segment in the fourth quarter of 2019 and first half of 2020; • volume increase in our Consumer Solutions segment mainly driven by our pool business; • volume increase in our residential and irrigation flow businesses in our Industrial & Flow Technologies segment; and • favorable foreign currency effects in 2020 compared to the prior year.

The 5.2 percent decrease in net sales for Industrial & Flow Technologies in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of decreased sales volume in our commercial water supply and water disposal, industrial filtration and food and beverage businesses due to less project sales as a result of customers deferring their capital spending and less service and aftermarket revenue driven by reduced consumption and travel restrictions from government-mandated shutdowns and 'shelter-in-place' orders due to the COVID-19 pandemic; and decreased sales volume in our residential and irrigation flow businesses in the first half of 2020 due to market conditions resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Organic growth is mixed with some segments growing and others declining.

factors.summary: The end market momentum is mixed, with the pool business experiencing strong growth due to increased consumer demand during the COVID-19 pandemic, while the commercial and industrial filtration segments face challenges and slowing momentum.

evidence.text: Within our Consumer Solutions segment, the COVID-19 pandemic has impacted demand in each of our businesses. Our pool business has experienced high demand as consumers sheltered-in-place and have spent more time at home.

The 8.1 percent increase in net sales for Consumer Solutions in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of increased sales volume across several of the product lines in our pool business due to consumers’ desire to invest in their pools and backyards while sheltering-in-place due to the COVID-19 pandemic; and higher sales volumes in our residential filtration product lines in North America and China during the second half of 2020.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed momentum with strong pool demand but challenges in other segments.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising due to inflation and supply chain disruptions, creating uncertainty about the timing and impact of market changes. While the company is attempting to offset these increases through pricing and productivity improvements, the current economic environment remains challenging.

evidence.text: We have experienced material and other cost inflation. We strive for productivity improvements, and we implement increases in selling prices to help mitigate this inflation. We expect the current economic environment will result in continuing price volatility for many of our raw materials, and we are uncertain as to the timing and impact of these market changes.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

evidence.text: The 0.4 percentage point increase in segment income for Consumer Solutions as a percentage of net sales in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of increased sales volume in our pool business; reduction in travel and entertainment, trade show and advertising expenses due to the COVID-19 pandemic and other cost reduction initiatives; impact of Aquion and Pelican acquisitions; and selective increases in selling prices to mitigate the impacts of inflation.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The company faces significant cost increases due to inflation and supply chain issues, despite efforts to mitigate through pricing and productivity improvements.

factors.summary: The company's margin trajectory is mixed, with some improvement in Consumer Solutions but overall pressure on gross margins due to cost increases, unfavorable mix, and other factors.

evidence.text: The 0.6 percentage point decrease in gross profit as a percentage of net sales in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of: • unfavorable mix within the pool and commercial and infrastructure flow businesses; • lower volumes within our commercial water supply, water disposal, industrial filtration and food and beverage businesses; • higher transportation and labor costs due to increased demand in our pool business; • costs related to providing for the health and safety of our employees specific to the COVID-19 pandemic of $8.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2020; and • inflationary increases related to certain raw materials.

The 0.4 percentage point increase in segment income for Consumer Solutions as a percentage of net sales in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of increased sales volume in our pool business; reduction in travel and entertainment, trade show and advertising expenses due to the COVID-19 pandemic and other cost reduction initiatives; impact of Aquion and Pelican acquisitions; and selective increases in selling prices to mitigate the impacts of inflation.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance with some improvement in Consumer Solutions but overall pressure on gross margins.

factors.summary: Cost reduction actions are being implemented, including temporary furloughs, hiring freezes, renegotiation with suppliers, and reductions in non-essential expenses, to manage expenses and deliver measurable savings.

evidence.text: With the continuing uncertainty in light of the COVID-19 pandemic, we have taken steps across our organization to align costs with lower sales volumes. These steps include renegotiation with suppliers to reduce input costs, driving manufacturing direct labor reductions in line with volume drop, delaying, reducing or eliminating purchased services and travel and, where appropriate, temporary furloughs and hiring freezes. Additionally, we are proactively managing our working capital and reviewing our capital spending plan, but have not deferred strategic ongoing initiatives.

The 0.4 percentage point increase in segment income for Consumer Solutions as a percentage of net sales in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of increased sales volume in our pool business; reduction in travel and entertainment, trade show and advertising expenses due to the COVID-19 pandemic and other cost reduction initiatives; impact of Aquion and Pelican acquisitions; and selective increases in selling prices to mitigate the impacts of inflation.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively implementing cost reduction measures to manage expenses effectively.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include ongoing impacts from the COVID-19 pandemic, supply chain disruptions, economic uncertainty, and reduced demand for products due to decreased sales volumes and customer deferrals of capital spending. These risks are exacerbated by government-mandated shutdowns and travel restrictions, leading to challenges in certain markets.

evidence.text: The current COVID-19 pandemic or continued spread of COVID-19 has caused a global economic slowdown, and a possibility of a global recession. In the event of a recession, demand for our products would decline and our business and results of operations would be adversely affected.

The 5.2 percent decrease in net sales for Industrial & Flow Technologies in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of decreased sales volume in our commercial water supply and water disposal, industrial filtration and food and beverage businesses due to less project sales as a result of customers deferring their capital spending and less service and aftermarket revenue driven by reduced consumption and travel restrictions from government-mandated shutdowns and 'shelter-in-place' orders due to the COVID-19 pandemic; and decreased sales volume in our residential and irrigation flow businesses in the first half of 2020 due to market conditions resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: very_negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant near-term risks including the COVID-19 pandemic, supply chain disruptions, and economic uncertainty.

factors.summary: The company has a clear long-term strategic direction focused on delivering core growth, managing price/cost inflation, and pursuing mergers and acquisitions. They are confident in their ability to execute these strategies, supported by sufficient liquidity and a strong commitment to maintaining investment grade ratings.

evidence.text: In 2021, our operating objectives remain to focus on delivering our core while continuing to build out our future. We expect to execute these objectives by: • Delivering revenue growth in our core businesses; • Delivering income and cash by managing price/cost inflation, prioritization of growth investments and addressing the cost structures as necessary; • Continued focus on capital allocation through: ◦ Commitment to maintain our investment grade rating; ◦ Return cash to shareholders through dividends and buybacks; and ◦ Supplement our business with strategically-aligned mergers and acquisitions.
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

evidence.text: We believe our existing liquidity position, coupled with our currently anticipated operating cash flows, will be sufficient to meet our cash needs arising in the ordinary course of business for the next twelve months. Although the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results is uncertain, we believe we are well-positioned to manage our business and have the ability and sufficient capacity to meet these cash requirements by using available cash, internally generated funds and borrowing under our committed and uncommitted credit facilities. We are committed to maintaining investment grade credit ratings and a solid liquidity position.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear long-term strategic direction with confidence in execution, supported by strong financial positioning.

factors.summary: The company identifies several key sensitivities, including commodity prices, interest rates, volumes, foreign exchange (FX), and government budgets, which could impact its near-term results. Additionally, the company's net sales were negatively affected by decreased sales volumes and foreign exchange movements, particularly in its Industrial & Flow Technologies segment, due to factors such as the COVID-19 pandemic and market conditions.

evidence.text: What variables (commodity prices, interest rates, volumes, FX, government budgets) does management identify as having the largest potential impact on near-term results?
evidence.subsection: Key Trends and Uncertainties Regarding Our Existing Business

evidence.text: The 5.2 percent decrease in net sales for Industrial & Flow Technologies in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of decreased sales volume in our commercial water supply and water disposal, industrial filtration and food and beverage businesses due to less project sales as a result of customers deferring their capital spending and less service and aftermarket revenue driven by reduced consumption and travel restrictions from government-mandated shutdowns and 'shelter-in-place' orders due to the COVID-19 pandemic; and decreased sales volume in our residential and irrigation flow businesses in the first half of 2020 due to market conditions resulting from the COVID-19 pandemic.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Exposure to key sensitivities without a clear positive or negative directional signal.

factors.summary: Economic conditions present a mixed outlook, with significant headwinds due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic, including the possibility of a global recession and reduced demand for products. However, the company believes it is well-positioned to manage its business, with sufficient liquidity and financial resources to navigate these challenges.

evidence.text: The current COVID-19 pandemic or continued spread of COVID-19 has caused a global economic slowdown, and a possibility of a global recession. In the event of a recession, demand for our products would decline and our business and results of operations would be adversely affected.

Although the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on our future results is uncertain, we believe we are well-positioned to manage our business and have the ability and sufficient capacity to meet these cash requirements by using available cash, internally generated funds and borrowing under our committed and uncommitted credit facilities. We are committed to maintaining investment grade credit ratings and a solid liquidity position.

The severity, magnitude and duration of the current COVID-19 pandemic is uncertain, rapidly changing and hard to predict. In 2020, the COVID-19 pandemic significantly impacted economic activity and markets around the world and our business, and it is expected to negatively impact our business in numerous ways, including but not limited to those outlined below:
evidence.subsection: The COVID-19 pandemic is expected to have a material negative impact on our business, financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed economic outlook with challenges from the pandemic but also signs of resilience.

factors.summary: The company actively engages in mergers and acquisitions as part of its growth strategy. Recent acquisitions, such as Aquion and Pelican, have contributed to increased net sales and are considered accretive to value creation.

evidence.text: In February 2019, as part of Consumer Solutions, we completed the acquisitions of Aquion, Inc. (''Aquion'') and Pelican Water Systems (''Pelican'') for $163.4 million and $121.1 million, respectively, in cash, net of cash acquired and final working capital true-ups. Aquion offers a diverse line of water conditioners, water filters, drinking-water purifiers, ozone and ultraviolet disinfection systems, reverse osmosis systems and acid neutralizers for the residential and commercial water treatment industry. Pelican provides residential whole home water treatment systems.

The 8.1 percent increase in net sales for Consumer Solutions in 2020 from 2019 was primarily the result of increased sales due to the Aquion and Pelican acquisitions that occurred in February 2019 and other small acquisitions in the fourth quarter of 2019 and during 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Active mergers and acquisitions contributing positively to net sales and value creation.
